hypothesis: Jointly learning a differentiable potential-energy surrogate Eφ(x) that is trained together with the AMDEN score network and integrated into a Boltzmann-consistent denoising objective, such that the reverse diffusion samples follow p(x) ∝ exp(−Eφ(x)), will enable standard denoising to generate low-energy, relaxation-consistent amorphous materials across melt/quench/anneal regimes without Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement.
